2603.13406.title: Nuanced Emotion Recognition Based on a Segment-based MLLM Framework Leveraging Qwen3-Omni for AH Detection
2603.13406.paper_summary: Emotion recognition in videos is a pivotal task in affective computing, where identifying subtle psychological states such as Ambivalence and Hesitancy holds significant value for behavioral intervention and digital health. Ambivalence and Hesitancy states often manifest through cross-modal inconsistencies such as discrepancies between facial expressions, vocal tones, and textual semantics, posing a substantial challenge for automated recognition. This paper proposes a recognition framework that integrates temporal segment modeling with Multimodal Large Language Models. To address computational efficiency and token constraints in long video processing, we employ a segment-based strategy, partitioning videos into short clips with a maximum duration of 5 seconds. We leverage the Qwen3-Omni-30B-A3B model, fine-tuned on the BAH dataset using LoRA and full-parameter strategies via the MS-Swift framework, enabling the model to synergistically analyze visual and auditory signals. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves an accuracy of 85.1% on the test set, significantly outperforming existing benchmarks and validating the superior capability of Multimodal Large Language Models in capturing complex and nuanced emotional conflicts. The code is released at https://github.com/dlnn123/A-H-Detection-with-Qwen-Omni.git.
2603.13406.paper_summary_zh: 情感識別在視頻中是情感計算中的一項關鍵任務，識別如矛盾和猶豫等微妙的心理狀態對於行為干預和數字健康具有重要價值。矛盾和猶豫狀態通常通過跨模態不一致性表現出來，例如面部表情、聲調和文本語義之間的差異，這對於自動識別構成了重大挑戰。本文提出了一個識別框架，將時間段建模與多模態大型語言模型相結合。為了解決長視頻處理中的計算效率和標記限制，我們採用基於段的策略，將視頻劃分為最大持續時間為5秒的短片段。我們利用Qwen3-Omni-30B-A3B模型，該模型在BAH數據集上使用LoRA和全參數策略進行微調，通過MS-Swift框架使模型能夠協同分析視覺和聽覺信號。實驗結果表明，所提出的方法在測試集上達到了85.1%的準確率，顯著超越了現有基準，驗證了多模態大型語言模型在捕捉複雜和微妙情感衝突方面的卓越能力。代碼已發布於 https://github.com/dlnn123/A-H-Detection-with-Qwen-Omni.git。
2603.13406.author: Liang Tang et.al.
2603.13406.authors: Liang Tang, Hongda Li, Jiayu Zhang, Long Chen, Shuxian Li, Siqi Pei, Tiaonan Duan, Yuhao Cheng
